question_text:
Palindromic Substrings

Given a string s, return the number of palindromic substrings in it.

A string is a palindrome when it reads the same backward as forward.

A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.
output_format: A single integer representing the count of palindromic substrings.
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters.
explanation: We use the "expand around center" technique. For each index `i`, we attempt two expansions: one for odd-length palindromes (center at `i`) and one for even-length palindromes (center between `i` and `i+1`). Each expansion moves outward while characters match, incrementing our palindrome count for every valid expansion step. This naturally counts every palindromic substring exactly once. The time complexity is O(N^2) in the worst case (e.g., all same characters), with O(1) auxiliary space.
